title: Scenario 1 · Ambiguous request
subtitle: See how the answer changes for different audiences and detail levels.
goal: Try A02 and A03 in a low-risk work question.
walkthroughSteps: Choose the first prompt and try one audience setting.
Send the prompt, read the answer, and notice the level of detail.
Use Next step to move to the second prompt and compare the response.

placeholderCards.label: A02 · Match the answer to the audience
placeholderCards.description: Switch between New hire, Manager, and Specialist to change the wording and focus of the reply.

placeholderCards.label: A03 · Change detail level per prompt
placeholderCards.description: Choose how short, guided, or thorough the next answer should be.

placeholderCards.label: What to notice
placeholderCards.description: Check whether the answer feels easier to use when both the audience and detail level fit the task.

title: Scenario 2 · Sensitive request
subtitle: Review the request first, then show a clear warning before answering.
goal: Try A06 and A09 in a request that needs caution.
walkthroughSteps: Choose a sensitive prompt and review the short setup card.
Optionally open the full setup and change one or more dropdowns.
Send the prompt and check whether the warning and the final answer feel clear and useful.

placeholderCards.label: A06 · Check the request before answering
placeholderCards.description: The assistant shows a short setup card first and lets you edit it if needed.

placeholderCards.label: A09 · Sensitive request warning
placeholderCards.description: You can compare a simple warning with a warning that also offers a review step.

placeholderCards.label: Extra cues inside the warning
placeholderCards.description: This scenario also hints at uncertainty and freshness without showing them as separate tools.

title: Scenario 3 · Evidence-heavy request
subtitle: Open the Sources view to inspect what supported the answer.
goal: Try A08 and see whether the evidence makes the answer easier to trust.
walkthroughSteps: Choose a prompt and send it to get an evidence-based answer.
Click a used, omitted, or inaccessible source to inspect the details.

placeholderCards.label: A08 · Sources panel
placeholderCards.description: The reply includes a Sources button that opens the evidence directly inside the message.

placeholderCards.label: Confidence cue
placeholderCards.description: The sources panel also shows how certain the assistant is about the answer.

placeholderCards.label: Freshness cue
placeholderCards.description: The sources panel also shows whether the supporting information is current.

title: Scenario 4 · Output generation
subtitle: Open an editable work output instead of receiving chat text only.
goal: Try A05 through one document flow and one presentation flow.
walkthroughSteps: Choose the document prompt or the presentation prompt.
Send the prompt to open the editor inside the chat.
Make a small change and export the result.

placeholderCards.label: A05 · Work output editor
placeholderCards.description: The assistant can return an editable document or slide deck instead of only a text reply.

placeholderCards.label: Presentation flow
placeholderCards.description: Edit slide content, add visuals, and export the deck as a PPTX file.

placeholderCards.label: Document flow
placeholderCards.description: Edit page content, place images, and export the result as a DOCX file.
